title: FastAPI: Pydantic validation errors
solution_steps: 1. Check the request body matches the Pydantic model schema
3. Use `example` in Field() for sample data in docs
4. Use `validator` decorator for custom validation logic
7. Use `request.json()` to inspect raw incoming body

title: Django: CSRF token missing or incorrect
solution_steps: 1. Add `{% csrf_token %}` inside all POST form tags
2. For AJAX: get token from cookie and set `X-CSRFToken` header
3. Use `@csrf_exempt` decorator for API views (not recommended)
5. For REST APIs: use token-based auth instead of CSRF

title: FastAPI: CORS errors with frontend
solution_steps: 2. Add allowed origins, methods, and headers
code_example:
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=["http://localhost:5173"],
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)

title: Flask: Application context errors
solution_steps: 1. Wrap code in `app.app_context()` when accessing `current_app` outside request
3. Use `g` proxy only within request context
4. Use `@app.before_request` to set up per-request state
5. For background tasks: pass `app` explicitly, don't access `current_app`
code_example:
from flask import current_app
def send_email_async(app, to, subject):
    with app.app_context():
        mail = current_app.extensions['mail']
        mail.send_message(subject, recipients=[to], body='...')

# In route:
Thread(target=send_email_async, args=(app, user.email, 'Welcome')).start()

title: SQLAlchemy: session management and detached instances
solution_steps: 1. Keep session open while accessing lazy-loaded attributes
3. Use `joinedload()` or `selectinload()` to eager load relationships
5. Use Flask-SQLAlchemy or FastAPI dependency for per-request sessions
6. Use `session.refresh(obj)` for up-to-date data from DB
code_example:
from sqlalchemy.orm import selectinload
stmt = select(User).options(selectinload(User.posts))
user = await session.execute(stmt)
# Posts are eagerly loaded â€” accessible after session close
user.posts  # No DetachedInstanceError

title: FastAPI: async SQLAlchemy sessions in dependencies
error_patterns: can't use sync session in async
solution_steps: 4. Use `selectinload` for eager loading in async mode
5. Don't mix sync and async sessions in same request
code_example:
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession, create_async_engine
from sqlalchemy.orm import selectinload

async def get_db() -> AsyncSession:
    async with AsyncSession(engine) as session:
        yield session

@app.get('/users/{id}')
async def get_user(id: int, db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)):
    result = await db.execute(select(User).options(selectinload(User.posts)))

title: Django: migrations conflicts
solution_steps: 3. Resolve by merging: `python manage.py makemigrations --merge` will prompt
5. To reset single app: delete migration files and `python manage.py makemigrations`
code_example:
python manage.py makemigrations --merge
# Review the merge migration
python manage.py migrate

# If totally stuck: (backup DB first!)
python manage.py migrate <app> zero
# Then re-migrate

title: Celery: task not executing (no worker)
code_example:
from celery import Celery
app = Celery('tasks', broker='redis://localhost:6379/0')
@app.task
def add(x, y):
    return x + y

# In code:
result = add.delay(4, 4)
print(result.get(timeout=5))

title: FastAPI: WebSocket connection handling
Django: N+1 query problem in ORM
solution_steps: 3. Use `only()` / `defer()` to select only needed columns
5. Use `Prefetch` object for advanced prefetch filtering

title: Flask: template not found / Jinja2 errors
solution_steps: 1. Check template path (default: `templates/` folder in app root)
2. Use absolute path for custom template folder
4. For syntax errors, Jinja2 shows line number
5. Use `{% raw %}` for literal Jinja2 delimiters

title: FastAPI: background tasks and lifespan
solution_steps: 4. Use Celery/ARQ for heavy background processing
5. Background tasks run after response is sent (no access to request)
code_example:
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager

@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    # Startup
    await db.connect()
    yield
    # Shutdown
    await db.disconnect()

app = FastAPI(lifespan=lifespan)

title: Django: static files not loading in production
solution_steps: 3. Add `whitenoise` to middleware for serving static files
6. Use `{% load static %}` and `{% static 'path' %}` in templates
code_example:
MIDDLEWARE = [
    'django.middleware.security.SecurityMiddleware',
    'whitenoise.middleware.WhiteNoiseMiddleware',  # After SecurityMiddleware
    ...
]
STATIC_ROOT = BASE_DIR / 'staticfiles'
STATICFILES_STORAGE = 'whitenoise.storage.CompressedManifestStaticFilesStorage'

title: SQLAlchemy: dialect and driver not installed
solution_steps: 1. Install required driver for your database
code_example:
# PostgreSQL async
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import create_async_engine
engine = create_async_engine(
    'postgresql+asyncpg://user:pass@localhost:5432/mydb'
)

# MySQL sync
engine = create_engine('mysql+pymysql://user:pass@localhost/mydb')

title: FastAPI: dependency caching and re-use
solution_steps: 1. Dependencies are cached within the same request scope
2. Same `Depends()` call across multiple routes = separate instances
3. Use `lru_cache` on dependency factory for singleton at module level
5. Dependencies can have their own sub-dependencies

title: Django: custom user model after migration
solution_steps: 3. If already migrated: dump data, drop DB, set model, migrate fresh
code_example:
# models.py
from django.contrib.auth.models import AbstractUser
class CustomUser(AbstractUser):
    phone_number = models.CharField(max_length=20, blank=True)

# settings.py
AUTH_USER_MODEL = 'accounts.CustomUser'

# admin.py
from django.contrib import admin
from .models import CustomUser
admin.site.register(CustomUser)

title: Celery: task ETA and countdown not working
solution_steps: 3. Celery Beat for periodic tasks: `celery -A app beat`
code_example:
# Schedule with countdown
add.apply_async((4, 4), countdown=60)

# Schedule with eta
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
add.apply_async((4, 4), eta=datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(minutes=5))

# Periodic task
@app.on_after_configure.connect
def setup_periodic_tasks(sender, **kwargs):
    sender.add_periodic_task(300, clean_old_records.s(), name='clean every 5 min')

title: FastAPI: file upload handling
solution_steps: 2. Read with `await file.read()` for small files
4. Set max size: `python-multipart` and uvicorn settings
5. Validate file type (MIME) and extension server-side
6. Save with `aiofiles.open()` for async file writing

title: Django: database connection timeout
solution_steps: 3. Use connection pooling (pgbouncer for Postgres, mysql.connector pool)
5. Check firewall and database host accessibility

title: FastAPI: error handling and exception middleware
solution_steps: 4. Use `from starlette.exceptions import HTTPException as StarletteHTTPException`
5. Add request ID to each error for traceability

title: SQLAlchemy: Alembic migration generation
error_patterns: Target database is not up to date
code_example:
# alembic/env.py
from app.models import Base  # Your SQLAlchemy Base
target_metadata = Base.metadata

# Commands
alembic init alembic
# Edit alembic.ini with sqlalchemy.url
alembic revision --autogenerate -m 'create users table'
alembic upgrade head
# Check status
alembic current

title: Django: DEBUG=False static/media issues
error_patterns: You may need to add '.*' to ALLOWED_HOSTS
solution_steps: 4. Serve media files via web server (nginx, Caddy, S3) in production
5. Run `collectstatic` after any static file change
6. Use `django-storages` for cloud storage (S3, GCS, Azure)
code_example:
ALLOWED_HOSTS = ['.example.com', 'localhost']
STATIC_ROOT = BASE_DIR / 'staticfiles'
MEDIA_ROOT = BASE_DIR / 'media'
MEDIA_URL = '/media/'

# For local dev with DEBUG=False:
from django.conf.urls.static import static
urlpatterns += static(settings.MEDIA_URL, document_root=settings.MEDIA_ROOT)

title: FastAPI: rate limiting with slowapi
solution_steps: 4. Use key functions: by IP, by user, or custom
5. Store limits in memory (default) or Redis for distributed apps
6. Handle rate limit exceeded responses gracefully

title: Django REST Framework: serialization depth
solution_steps: 1. Set `depth = 1` in Meta class for limited nesting
4. Create separate read/write serializers for nested data

title: Celery: chord/callback not working
solution_steps: 1. Use `group(task.s(...) for i in items) | callback.s()` for chord
4. Use `result.get()` on group to wait for all results
5. Callback receives list of group results as argument
